label: Parcel ID (RPC)
description: The county's Real Property Code — a unique identifier for each parcel in Arlington's tax and property records.
source: Arlington County GIS parcels

label: Street Address
description: The property's mailing address, formatted as street number and name, city, and ZIP code. Useful for looking up the property on a map or in county records.
source: Arlington Open Data — Real Estate Property API

label: Civic Association / Neighborhood
description: The civic association area the parcel falls within. Arlington uses civic associations as its primary neighborhood unit.
source: Arlington County GIS — Civic Association Polygons

label: Zoning District
description: The official zoning code assigned to this parcel. Determines what can be built by-right (without special permits). Residential districts include R-5, R-6, R-8, R-10, R2-7, R15-30T, etc.
source: Arlington County GIS — Zoning Polygons

label: Split Zoning?
description: True if the parcel straddles two or more zoning districts. Analysis uses the primary (centroid) zone; results should be treated with extra caution for split-zoned parcels.
source: Calculated from spatial join

label: Lot Area (sq ft)
description: The total area of the parcel in square feet, calculated from the GIS parcel boundary. This is the authoritative geometric area, which may differ slightly from the county's recorded lot size.
source: Calculated from GIS parcel geometry (EPSG:2283)

label: Lot Width (ft)
description: The estimated width of the parcel in feet, calculated using the minimum bounding rectangle of the parcel shape. For irregular lots this is an approximation.
source: Calculated from GIS parcel geometry

label: Meets Zoning Minimums?
description: True if the lot meets the minimum area and width requirements for its zoning district. Non-conforming lots may have limited by-right development options.
source: Calculated from lot metrics vs. zoning rules

label: Year Built
description: The year the current structure was built, according to county property records. Blank for vacant parcels.
source: Arlington Open Data — Real Estate Property API

label: Property Type
description: The county's classification of the existing use of the property (e.g. Single Family Detached, Townhouse, Vacant Residential).
source: Arlington Open Data — Real Estate Property API

label: Current Building Size (sq ft)
description: The gross floor area of the building currently on the parcel, in square feet, from county records. This is total finished floor area across all stories. Blank for vacant parcels.
source: Arlington Open Data — Real Estate Property API

label: Current Stories
description: Number of stories in the existing building. Used to estimate the building footprint when combined with gross floor area.
source: Arlington Open Data — Real Estate Property API

label: Assessed Land Value ($)
description: The county's most recent assessed value of the land only (not the building). Used as a key input to the valuation estimates.
source: Arlington Open Data — Real Estate Assessment API (2025–2026)

label: Assessed Building Value ($)
description: The county's most recent assessed value of the building (improvements) on the parcel. A low or zero improvement value often indicates a vacant lot or a teardown candidate.
source: Arlington Open Data — Real Estate Assessment API (2025–2026)

label: Max Building Footprint Allowed (sq ft)
description: The largest building footprint (ground-floor area) allowed by zoning rules for this parcel, in square feet. Determined by the zoning district's lot coverage and footprint percentage limits.
source: Calculated from lot area × zoning district standards

label: Max Buildable Floor Area (sq ft)
description: An estimate of the maximum total floor area that could be built on this parcel by-right, in square feet. Calculated as max footprint × 2.5 stories (the typical assumption for 35-ft height limit residential districts). This is an estimate — actual buildable area depends on setbacks and design.
source: Calculated: max_footprint_sf × 2.5 stories

label: Max Height Allowed (ft)
description: The maximum building height permitted by zoning, in feet.
source: Arlington County Zoning Ordinance — residential district standards

label: Unused Floor Area Capacity (sq ft)
description: How much additional floor area could be built on this parcel before reaching the zoning maximum — in square feet. Calculated as max buildable GFA minus current building GFA. Negative values mean the existing building exceeds what current zoning would allow (grandfathered / legal nonconforming).
source: Calculated: max_gfa_sf − current_gfa_sf

label: How Built-Out Is This Parcel? (%)
description: What percentage of the maximum allowed floor area is already built. 0% means vacant; 100% means fully built to the zoning limit; over 100% means the existing building exceeds current zoning (grandfathered).
source: Calculated: current_gfa_sf ÷ max_gfa_sf × 100

label: Development Status
description: A simple classification of how developed this parcel is relative to its zoning potential. 'vacant' = no building; 'underdeveloped' = built to less than 80% of zoning limit; 'near-capacity' = built to 80–100% of limit; 'overdeveloped' = existing building exceeds current zoning limit (grandfathered); 'built-no-data' = a building exists (confirmed by year-built record) but floor area cannot be estimated because the assessment API has no record for this parcel — excluded from available-rights and valuation calculations.
source: Derived from gfa_utilization_pct, is_vacant, and gfa_source

label: Building Size Data Source
description: How the current building floor area was determined. 'property_api' = reported directly by the county property records (typical for commercial); 'estimated_from_improvement_value' = estimated from the county's assessed building value divided by a $/SF rate (typical for single-family residential); 'building_detected_no_gfa_data' = a building is confirmed by year-built records but floor area cannot be estimated because the assessment API has no data for this parcel (data quality issue); 'not_available' = no building data found.
source: Derived from Arlington Open Data APIs during analysis

label: Estimated Development Value — Low ($)
description: The low end of the estimated market value of this parcel's unused development capacity, in dollars. Uses the conservative discount factor from the land residual method. This is an estimate for policy analysis, NOT a property appraisal.
source: Calculated using the land residual method

label: Estimated Development Value — High ($)
description: The high end of the estimated market value of this parcel's unused development capacity, in dollars. Uses the aggressive discount factor from the land residual method.
source: Calculated using the land residual method

label: Spot Check Result
description: The outcome of a manual verification of this parcel's classification. Common values: 'confirmed' = analysis matches ground truth; 'incorrect' = analysis is wrong; 'vacant - confirmed buildable' = empty lot verified as developable. Blank if the parcel has not been spot-checked.
source: Manual verification recorded in spot_checks.csv

label: Spot Check Notes
description: Free-text notes from the manual verification of this parcel. May include observations about the property, data discrepancies, or recommended follow-up actions.
source: Manual verification recorded in spot_checks.csv
example: Large home and outbuilding visible on satellite imagery

label: Spot Check Date
description: The date the manual verification was performed (YYYY-MM-DD).
source: Manual verification recorded in spot_checks.csv
